title: MAGI Recon Checklist
description: Practical, local-first MAGI checklist for passive recon, scanning, service review, and web exposure validation.

items.title: Validate target scope
items.guide_text: Confirm the target hostname, domain, IP, or CIDR is explicitly in scope before starting passive or active collection.
items.example_command: Document scope in notes and validate the target string before execution.

items.title: Run WHOIS lookup
items.guide_text: Collect registrar, name server, and registration context as baseline passive recon for the scoped domain.

items.title: Resolve core DNS records
items.guide_text: Resolve A records and baseline DNS information for the scoped domain before expanding the footprint.

items.title: Discover subdomains with passive sources
items.guide_text: Use passive discovery sources to identify visible subdomains before deeper validation.

items.title: Resolve extended DNS records
items.guide_text: Resolve A, AAAA, CNAME, NS, MX, and TXT records for the scoped domain and store them as normalized findings.

items.title: Run host discovery
items.guide_text: Identify responsive hosts first and capture findings as structured data.

items.title: Probe live web services
items.guide_text: Check reachable HTTP(S) services, titles, status codes, IPs, and technology fingerprints for scoped targets.

items.title: Run port scanning
items.guide_text: Enumerate common exposed TCP ports on live hosts before deeper service review.

items.title: Run service enumeration
items.guide_text: Enumerate open services and versions on in-scope hosts and store parsed output from Nmap XML.

items.title: Run aggressive service review
items.guide_text: Use the aggressive Nmap profile only after basic service results are available and within scope.

items.title: Review nuclei exposure checks
items.guide_text: Run template-driven web checks against validated live hosts and store results for later triage.

items.title: Run chained web triage
items.guide_text: Discover subdomains, verify live web services, crawl where relevant, and run nuclei checks against reachable targets.

items.title: Review web paths and exposed assets
items.guide_text: Use Angel Eyes evidence from ffuf, Gobuster fallback runs, katana, httpx, and nuclei to review sensitive files, logs, repo metadata, and server configuration paths.

items.title: Record findings and next actions
items.guide_text: Capture validated findings, follow-up review work, and remediation notes in encrypted local storage and exported reports.
